Total no, of ways $=5 !=120$
Since all numbers are $>20,000$
$\therefore$ all numbers $2,3,5,7,9$ can come at first place.
$\mathrm{q}: \begin{array}{llllll}0 & 0 & 0 & 0 & 0 & \text { place } \\ 3 & 4 & 3 & 2 & 1 & \text { ways }\end{array}$
Total no. of ways $=3 \times 4 !=72$
( $\because 2$ and 9 can not be put at first place)
So, $p: q=120: 72=5: 3$

solution:
Given word 'VOWELS'

Number of words Arranging all the letters = 6!

In the given word Consonants are W, L, S.

Treating all the consonants as one unit then number of units will be 4.

No of such arrangements =4!.3!

VOWELS all the consonants never come together =Total - all consonants come together =6!-4!×3!=576

subject: Mathematics
solution:
Given: α=(4!)!(4!)3!, β=(5!)!(5!)4!

⇒α=(24)!(4!)6, β=(120)!(5!)24

If 24 distinct objects are divided into 6 groups of 4 objects in each group, then the number of ways of formation of group is=24!(4!)6.6!∈N

Similarly, let 120 distinct objects are divided into 24 groups of 5 objects in each group.

So, the number of ways of formation of groups

=(120)!(5!)24·24!∈N

solution: $\begin{aligned} & =\text { Total }-\left[\left(\mathrm{All} \mathrm{in}_1 \text { and } \mathrm{R}_3\right)+\left(\text { All in } \mathrm{R}_2 \text { and } \mathrm{R}_3\right)+\right. \\ & \left.\left(\text { All in } \mathrm{R}_1 \text { and } \mathrm{R}_2\right)\right] \\ & ={ }^8 \mathrm{C}_5 \cdot \mid 5-\left\{\underline{5}+\left\lfloor 5+{ }^6 \mathrm{C}_5 \cdot \underline{5}\right\}\right. \\ & =\lfloor 5(56-1-1-6)=120(48) \\ & =5760\end{aligned}$

subject: Mathematics
solution:
Three persons must exit at three different floors.
The lift goes to the 10th floor and does not stop at floors 1, 2, and 3.
Available floors for exit: 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, 10 (7 floors total).
The number of ways to assign 3 persons to 3 different floors from 7 available floors is a permutation problem: $P(7, 3) = 7 \times 6 \times 5 = 210$.
Alternatively, choose 3 floors from 7 and arrange 3 persons: ${}^{7}P_{3} = 35 \times 6 = 210$.

subject: Mathematics
solution:
Let the number of men participating in tournament =m.

Number of women participating in tournament =2.

Each player will play 2 games with every other player and two women will play 2 games with each man.

Total numbers of games played by women =2×2m=4m

Total number of games played by men =2·mC2

From given data

2· mC2-4m=66

⇒2mm-12-4m=66

⇒m2-5m-66=0

⇒m-11m+6=0

⇒m=11 or -6 but m∈N, m≠-6

⇒m∈10,12.

=5!1-11!+12!-13!+14!-15!

=5!-5!+60-20+5-1

=44

=6×6×6×6=1296

Since, 2+6+6+6+6=1296

Number of five-digit numbers formed using the given digits with 3 at the first place is

=1296

Since, 3+6+6+6+6=1296

429 0+6=6

429 2 0=1

=1296×2+216+5×36+6+1+1+1=2997

subject: Mathematics
solution:
There are 5 vowels in the given word which are 4E's & 1I.

Since they have to always occur together we take them as a single object E E E E I for the time being.

This single object together with 7 remaining object will account for 8 objects.

There 8 objects in which there are 3N's & 2D's can be arrangement in 8!3!2! ways.

Corresponding to each of their arrangements the 5 vowels E,E,E,E & I which can be arranged in 5!4!

Hence, required number of arrangements.

=8!3!2!×5!4!=16800

Hence this is the correct option.

subject: Mathematics
solution:
Given,

7 boys and 5 girls are to be seated around a circular such that no two girls to be seated together,

Now we know that n objects can be arranged in a circle in (n-1)! ways.

Let us first arrange 7 boys in circular arrangement in 7-1! ways.

Now there will be 7 gaps.

So let us select any 5 gaps out of 7 gaps and arrange 5 girls in the chosen gaps. This can be done in C57×5! ways.

Hence, required arrangements are 6!×C57×5!

=6×5!×7×62×5!

=1265!2.

Therefore, required arrangements are 1265!2

subject: Mathematics
solution:
Given,

We have to form 6 digit number using the digits 4, 5 & 9 and for number should be divisible by 6 which means that the addition of number should be divisible by 3 and unit place should be even, so we will make following cases:

Case 1 The number is 444444 which can be arranged in 1 way,

Case 2 when the number is formed using the digits 4,4,4,5,9 and last place is fixed with 4, that can be arranged in 5!3!=20 ways

Case 3 when number is formed using the digits 4,4,5,5,5 and last place is fixed with 4, that can be arranged in 5!3!2!=10 ways

Case 4 when number is formed using the digits 4,4,9,9,9 and last place is fixed by 4, that can be arranged in 5!3!2!=10 ways

Case 5 when number is formed using the digits 4,5,5,9,9 and last place is fixed with 4, that can be arranged in 5!2!2!=30 ways

Case 6 when number is formed using the digits 5,9,9,9,9 and last place is fixed with 4, that can be arranged in 5!4!=5 ways

Case 7 when number is formed using the digits 5,5,5,5,9 and last place is fixed with 4, that can be arranged in 5!4!=5 ways

Now adding all the cases we get, 1+20+10+10+30+5+5=81 ways.

subject: Mathematics
solution:
We have to form a number greater than 10,000 by using the digits 0, 2, 4, 6, 8, hence, the first place of the number can't be zero.

Thus, for the first place we have 4 choices i.e. 2, 4, 6, 8 and the remaining places can be filled with any of the digits, thus we have respectively 4, 3, 2, 1 choices.

Hence, using the fundamental principle of multiplication, the total numbers which are greater than 10,000 are

=4×4×3×2=96.

subject: Mathematics
solution:
Exponent of 7 in $101!$ is:
$\left\lfloor \frac{101}{7} \right\rfloor + \left\lfloor \frac{101}{7^2} \right\rfloor + \left\lfloor \frac{101}{7^3}\right\rfloor + \ldots = 14 + 2 = 16$.

solution:
As we know that the product of numbers is even when at least one of the number must be even.

Hence total subsets of A in which product of numbers is even = Total subsets – total subsets in which all the elements are odd

=2100-250=250250-1.
